---
title: "01_power_simulation"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(brms)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(furrr)
plan(multisession, workers = 4) # Adjust to your CPU

# Define prior once — reused across all fits (saves compilation)
shared_prior <- c(
  prior(normal(0, 2),  class = b),
  prior(normal(0, 3),  class = Intercept)
)
```

```{r}
# 1. Function to simulate one study
simulate_study <- function(n_children = 1, n_sessions = 20) {
  expand_grid(
    child_id = factor(1:n_children),
    session = 1:n_sessions,
    prep = factor(c("No Prep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "Child Choice")),
    time_point = c("5min", "15min", "30min")
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      # Assume 'Child Choice' adds +0.5 to the latent engagement scale
      true_effect = ifelse(prep == "Child Choice", 0.5, 0),
      # ifelse(
      #   comparison == 'control',
      #   ifelse(prep == "Child Choice", 0.5, 0),
      #   case_when(
      #     prep == "No Prep" ~ 0.0,
      #     prep == "Calming" ~ 0.5,
      #     prep == "Stimulating" ~ 1.0,
      #     prep == "Child Choice" ~ 1.5,
      #     TRUE ~ 0
      #   )),
      # Add random intercept per child
      child_intercept = rnorm(n_children, 0, 0.5)[child_id],
      # Generate ordinal outcome (simplified logic)
      latent_score = true_effect + child_intercept + rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
      engagement = cut(latent_score, breaks = 5, labels = FALSE) %>% as.numeric()
    )
}

# 2. Parallel Power Analysis
run_power_analysis <- function(n_sims = 8, n_sessions = 12, comparison = 'control') {
  
  f <- function(i) {
    # Re-load libraries inside the worker if necessary
    library(brms)
    
    sim_data <- simulate_study(n_sessions = n_sessions)
    
    # Fit the model
    # Use 1 chain per sim to keep cores occupied 1:1 with sims
    fit <- brm(
      engagement ~ prep + (1 + prep | child_id),
      data = sim_data,
      family = cumulative(),
      chains = 1, iter = 1000, refresh = 0,
      silent = 2 # Reduces console clutter
    )
    print('Done with the fit')
    
    if (comparison == 'control') {
      post_summary <- as.data.frame(fixef(fit))
      # Check if Child Choice > No Prep (Reference)
      return(post_summary["prepChildChoice", "Q2.5"] > 0)
      
    } else if (comparison == 'pairwise') {
      # Test all consecutive pairs in the ladder
      test_pairs <- c(
        "prepChildChoice - prepCalming > 0",
        "prepChildChoice - prepStimulating > 0",
        "prepChildChoice > 0" # prepChildChoice vs No Prep (Intercept)
      )
      hyp_results <- hypothesis(fit, test_pairs)
      # Returns TRUE if all Evidence Ratios > 19 (95% probability)
      return(all(hyp_results$hypothesis$Evid.Ratio > 19))
    }
    print('Done with the loop')
  }
  
  paralel_run = FALSE
  
  if (paralel_run) {
    library(dplyr)
    library(tidyr)
    library(future.apply)
      # Setup parallel workers
    plan(multisession, workers = 4)
    
    results <- future_lapply(1:n_sims, f, future.seed = TRUE)
    
    # Close workers
    plan(sequential)
    
    return(mean(unlist(results), na.rm = TRUE))
    
  } else {
    
     results <- c()
     for(i in 1:n_sims) { 
       is_significant <- f(i)
       results <- c(results, is_significant) 
     }
     return(mean(results))  
  }
  

}

final_power <- run_power_analysis(n_sims = 8, comparison = 'control')
print(paste("Estimated Power:", final_power))
```

```{r}
session_counts <- c(8, 12, 16, 20, 24)
power_results <- sapply(session_counts, function(s) run_power_analysis(n_sessions = s))

# Plotting the "Probability as a function of Sessions"
plot(session_counts, power_results, type = "b", 
     main = "Power Curve", xlab = "Sessions", ylab = "Power")
```

```{r}
# ── 1. Simulate one n-of-1 study ─────────────────────────────────────────────
simulate_single_child <- function(n_sessions = 20, effect_size = 0.5) {
  
  # Fixed thresholds: more realistic than data-driven breaks
  # Roughly maps a N(0,1) latent variable to a 1-5 ordinal scale
  thresholds <- c(-1.5, -0.5, 0.5, 1.5)
  
  expand_grid(
    session    = 1:n_sessions,
    prep       = factor(
      c("No Prep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "Child Choice"),
      levels = c("No Prep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "Child Choice")
    ),
    time_point = c("5min", "15min", "30min")
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      true_effect  = if_else(prep == "Child Choice", effect_size, 0),
      latent_score = true_effect + rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
      # Stable ordinal conversion using fixed thresholds
      engagement   = as.integer(
        cut(latent_score, breaks = c(-Inf, thresholds, Inf), labels = FALSE)
      )
    )
}

# ── 2. Detection criterion for one fitted model ───────────────────────────────
check_individual_effect <- function(fit, comparison = "control") {
  if (comparison == "control") {
    # 95% CI on Child Choice coefficient excludes zero
    ci <- fixef(fit)["prepChildChoice", c("Q2.5", "Q97.5")]
    return(ci["Q2.5"] > 0)
    
  } else if (comparison == "pairwise") {
    # Child Choice credibly beats every other condition
    hyp <- hypothesis(fit, c(
      "prepChildChoice > 0",                       # vs No Prep
      "prepChildChoice - prepCalming > 0",          # vs Calming
      "prepChildChoice - prepStimulating > 0"       # vs Stimulating
    ))
    # Evid.Ratio > 19 ≈ posterior probability > 0.95
    return(all(hyp$hypothesis$Evid.Ratio > 19))
  }
}

# ── 3. Power for one session count ───────────────────────────────────────────
power_for_n_sessions <- function(n_sessions, n_sims, effect_size, comparison,
                                 shared_prior) {
  cat(sprintf("\n=== %d sessions ===\n", n_sessions))
  
  detections <- map_lgl(seq_len(n_sims), function(i) {
    cat(sprintf("  sim %d/%d\r", i, n_sims))
    
    sim_data <- simulate_single_child(
      n_sessions  = n_sessions,
      effect_size = effect_size
    )
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      brm(
        engagement ~ prep,         # No random effects: single child
        data    = sim_data,
        family  = cumulative("logit"),
        prior   = shared_prior,
        chains  = 1, iter = 2000, warmup = 1000,
        refresh = 0, silent = 2,
        seed    = i * 1000 + n_sessions
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    
    if (is.null(fit)) return(NA)
    
    tryCatch(
      check_individual_effect(fit, comparison),
      error = function(e) NA
    )
  })
  
  tibble(
    n_sessions  = n_sessions,
    power       = mean(detections, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_detected  = sum(detections, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_valid     = sum(!is.na(detections))
  )
}

# ── 4. Session sweep ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
run_session_sweep <- function(
    session_range = c(5, 10, 15, 20, 30, 40),  # sessions per prep per time point
    n_sims        = 20,                          # increase to 50-100 for final run
    effect_size   = 0.5,
    comparison    = "control",
    target_power  = 0.95
) {
  results <- map_dfr(
    session_range,
    power_for_n_sessions,
    n_sims      = n_sims,
    effect_size = effect_size,
    comparison  = comparison,
    shared_prior = shared_prior
  )
  
  # ── Summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  cat("\n\n── Power by session count ──\n")
  print(results)
  
  # Minimum sessions reaching target
  min_sessions <- results %>%
    filter(power >= target_power) %>%
    slice_min(n_sessions, n = 1)
  
  if (nrow(min_sessions) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf(
      "\n⚠ No session count in range reached %.0f%% power. Extend session_range.\n",
      target_power * 100
    ))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf(
      "\n✓ Minimum sessions for %.0f%% power: %d\n",
      target_power * 100, min_sessions$n_sessions
    ))
  }
  
  # ── Plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  p <- ggplot(results, aes(x = n_sessions, y = power)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "#2c7bb6") +
    geom_point(size = 3,    colour = "#2c7bb6") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = target_power, linetype = "dashed",
               colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.8) +
    annotate("text", x = min(session_range), y = target_power + 0.02,
             label = paste0(target_power * 100, "% target"),
             colour = "firebrick", hjust = 0) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title    = "N-of-1 power analysis: sessions needed to detect individual prep effect",
      subtitle = sprintf("Effect size = %.1f | Comparison = %s | %d sims per point",
                         effect_size, comparison, n_sims),
      x = "Sessions per prep condition per time point",
      y = "Power"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  
  print(p)
  invisible(results)
}

# ── 5. Run ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sweep_results <- run_session_sweep(
  session_range = c(5, 10, 15, 20, 30),
  n_sims        = 20,          # bump to 50-100 for a final estimate
  effect_size   = 0.5,
  comparison    = "control"    # or "pairwise"
)
```

```{r}
simulate_study_full <- function(n_children = 25, n_sessions = 20, effect_size = 0.5) {
  
  thresholds <- c(-1.5, -0.5, 0.5, 1.5)
  
  # Child-level random effects (intercept + 3 prep slopes)
  child_rfx <- map_dfr(1:n_children, function(id) {
    re <- MASS::mvrnorm(1,
      mu    = rep(0, 4),
      Sigma = diag(c(0.5, 0.8, 0.8, 0.8))  # match your earlier variance assumptions
    )
    tibble(child_id = factor(id),
           re_intercept = re[1], re_calming = re[2],
           re_stimulating = re[3], re_childchoice = re[4])
  })
  
  session_level <- expand_grid(
    child_id = factor(1:n_children),
    session  = 1:n_sessions
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      prep = sample(
        factor(c("No Prep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "Child Choice"),
               levels = c("No Prep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "Child Choice")),
        n(), replace = TRUE
      ),
      baseline_state = sample(
        factor(c("Tired", "Neutral", "Hyper")), n(), replace = TRUE
      )
    )
  
  # Then expand over time points only
  expand_grid(
    session_level,
    time_point = c("5min", "15min", "30min")
  ) %>%
    left_join(child_rfx, by = "child_id") %>%
    mutate(
      # Fixed effects
      fe_prep = case_when(
        prep == "Child Choice" ~ effect_size,
        prep == "Calming"      ~ 0,
        prep == "Stimulating"  ~ 0,
        TRUE                   ~ 0
      ),
      fe_time      = if_else(time_point == "15min", 0.1, 0),  # small time effect
      fe_baseline  = case_when(
        baseline_state == "Tired" ~ -0.3,
        baseline_state == "Hyper" ~  0.2,
        TRUE                      ~  0
      ),
      # Interaction: Child Choice works better when Hyper, worse when Tired
      fe_interaction = if_else(
        prep == "Child Choice" & baseline_state == "Hyper", 0.2,
        if_else(prep == "Child Choice" & baseline_state == "Tired", -0.2, 0)
      ),
      # Random slopes applied per child
      re_prep = case_when(
        prep == "Calming"      ~ re_calming,
        prep == "Stimulating"  ~ re_stimulating,
        prep == "Child Choice" ~ re_childchoice,
        TRUE                   ~ 0
      ),
      latent_score = re_intercept + fe_prep + fe_time + fe_baseline + 
                     fe_interaction + re_prep + rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
      engagement   = as.integer(
        cut(latent_score, breaks = c(-Inf, thresholds, Inf), labels = FALSE)
      )
    ) %>%
    select(child_id, session, prep, time_point, baseline_state, engagement)
}

# ── 2. Detection criterion for one fitted model ───────────────────────────────
check_individual_effect <- function(fit, comparison = "control") {
  if (comparison == "control") {
    # 95% CI on Child Choice coefficient excludes zero
    ci <- fixef(fit)["prepChildChoice", c("Q2.5", "Q97.5")]
    return(ci["Q2.5"] > 0)
    
  } else if (comparison == "pairwise") {
    # Child Choice credibly beats every other condition
    hyp <- hypothesis(fit, c(
      "prepChildChoice > 0",                        # vs No Prep
      "prepChildChoice - prepCalming > 0",          # vs Calming
      "prepChildChoice - prepStimulating > 0"       # vs Stimulating
    ))
    # Evid.Ratio > 19 ≈ posterior probability > 0.95
    return(all(hyp$hypothesis$Evid.Ratio > 19))
  }
}

check_all_children_effects <- function(fit) {
  # 1. Extract child-specific estimates (Fixed + Random combined)
  # This returns a summary table for every child
  child_ests <- coef(fit)$child_id
  
  # 2. Extract the Lower Bound of the 95% Credible Interval for 'prepChildChoice'
  # Logic: child_id is the first dimension, Q2.5 is the summary stat, prepChildChoice is the parameter
  lower_cis <- child_ests[, "Q2.5", "prepChildChoice"]
  
  # 3. Calculate the percentage of children who have a "Significant" individualized effect
  # i.e., their 95% CI is entirely above zero
  proportion_detected <- mean(lower_cis > 0)
  
  return(proportion_detected)
}

# ── 3. Power for one session count ───────────────────────────────────────────
power_for_n_sessions <- function(n_sessions, n_sims, effect_size,
                                 shared_prior) {
  cat(sprintf("\n=== %d sessions ===\n", n_sessions))
  
  detections <- future_map_dbl(seq_len(n_sims), function(i) {
    cat(sprintf("  sim %d/%d\r", i, n_sims))
    
    sim_data <- simulate_study_full(
      n_sessions  = n_sessions,
      effect_size = effect_size
    )
    
    # print("simulate_study_full done")
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      brm(
        engagement ~ prep + 
          time_point +
          baseline_state +
          # prep:baseline_state + # overkill
         (1 + prep || child_id), # The double bar tells brms to treat the slopes as independent. This is usually sufficient for power analysis.
          data    = sim_data,
          family  = cumulative("logit"),
          prior   = shared_prior,
          chains  = 2, 
          iter    = 1000,
          warmup  = 500,
          backend = "cmdstanr",
          threads = threading(2),
          refresh = 0, 
          silent  = 2
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    
    # print("fit done")
    
    if (is.null(fit)) return(NA)
    
    tryCatch(
      # check_individual_effect(fit),
      check_all_children_effects(fit),
      error = function(e) NA
    )
  }, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE))
  
  print(mean(detections, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(tibble(
    n_sessions  = n_sessions,
    power       = mean(detections, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_detected  = sum(detections, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_valid     = sum(!is.na(detections))
  ))
}

# ── 4. Session sweep ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
run_session_sweep <- function(
    session_range = c(5, 10, 15, 20, 30, 40),  # sessions per prep per time point
    n_sims        = 20,                          # increase to 50-100 for final run
    effect_size   = 0.5,
    comparison    = "control",
    target_power  = 0.95
) {
  # Define prior once — reused across all fits (saves compilation)
  shared_prior <- c(
    prior(normal(0, 2),  class = b),
    prior(normal(0, 3),  class = Intercept)
  )
  
  results <- map_dfr(
    session_range,
    power_for_n_sessions,
    n_sims      = n_sims,
    effect_size = effect_size,
    shared_prior = shared_prior
  )
  
  # ── Summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  cat("\n\n── Power by session count ──\n")
  print(results)
  
  # Minimum sessions reaching target
  min_sessions <- results %>%
    filter(power >= target_power) %>%
    slice_min(n_sessions, n = 1)
  
  if (nrow(min_sessions) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf(
      "\n⚠ No session count in range reached %.0f%% power. Extend session_range.\n",
      target_power * 100
    ))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf(
      "\n✓ Minimum sessions for %.0f%% power: %d\n",
      target_power * 100, min_sessions$n_sessions
    ))
  }
  
  # ── Plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  p <- ggplot(results, aes(x = n_sessions, y = power)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "#2c7bb6") +
    geom_point(size = 3,    colour = "#2c7bb6") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = target_power, linetype = "dashed",
               colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.8) +
    annotate("text", x = min(session_range), y = target_power + 0.02,
             label = paste0(target_power * 100, "% target"),
             colour = "firebrick", hjust = 0) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title    = "N-of-1 power analysis: sessions needed to detect individual prep effect",
      subtitle = sprintf("Effect size = %.1f | Comparison = %s | %d sims per point",
                         effect_size, comparison, n_sims),
      x = "Sessions per child",
      y = "Power"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  
  print(p)
  invisible(results)
}

# ── 5. Run ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sweep_results <- run_session_sweep(
  session_range = c(20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  n_sims        = 10,          # bump to 50-100 for a final estimate
  effect_size   = 0.5,
  comparison    = "control"    # or "pairwise"
)
```

```{r}


sim_data <- simulate_study_full(
  n_sessions  = 40,
  effect_size = 0.5
)

print("simulate_study_full done")

fit <- tryCatch(
  brm(
    engagement ~ prep + 
      time_point +
      baseline_state +
      # prep:baseline_state + # overkill
      (1 + prep || child_id), # The double bar tells brms to treat the slopes as independent. This is usually sufficient for power analysis.
    data    = sim_data,
    family  = cumulative("logit"),
    prior   = shared_prior,
    chains  = 1, 
    iter    = 100,
    warmup  = 50,
    backend = "cmdstanr",     # Use the new engine
    threads = threading(4),   # Use 4 threads per chain
    refresh = 0, 
    silent  = 2
  ),
  error = function(e) NULL
)

print("fit done")

if (is.null(fit)) return(NA)

tryCatch(
  check_individual_effect(fit),
  error = function(e) NA
)

print("check_individual_effect done")
```

```{r}
check_individual_effect(fit)
```
